judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit every expectation with the same grounded evidence; they are comparable, with Report 1 marginally firmer on removing the AI-assistance footer.

report_1.evidence: "Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF" — feature does not exist; remove. ... There is no export endpoint anywhere in the project ... "Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default" — false; correct or remove. `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`

report_2.evidence: the feature does not exist. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` ... This entire section must be removed. ... `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` ... Correct it to "opt-in" or remove it.

report_1.evidence: `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices` (versus `/schedule` for the "before") ... `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES". So the document shows customers a billing/invoices page as the "redesigned calendar".

report_2.evidence: `img/after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES". The capture manifest confirms ... maps `before_scheduling.png` to route `/schedule` but `after_scheduling.png` to route `/billing/invoices`. Customers would be shown a billing screen as the "redesigned calendar."

report_1.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **No** | Missing file ... | — (not referenced) | `img/unused_capture_07.png` | Orphan file rendering "PATIENT LIST". Unused by the document

report_2.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **no** | missing ... | — (not referenced) | `img/unused_capture_07.png` | orphan file showing "Patient List"; referenced nowhere in the doc or the manifest

report_1.evidence: The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33). This is an internal drafting note, not customer content. ... Remove it

report_2.evidence: The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33). This is internal drafting/attribution metadata, not customer content ... but as written, a footer calling the document a "draft" definitely cannot go out in a customer email.
